seoTitle: Federal vs Fern Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Federal and Fern Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Federal vs Fern Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Federal and Fern Bay.
heading: Federal vs Fern Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Federal, New South Wales and Fern Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Federal has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Fern Bay (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Federal or Fern Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Federal has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Fern Bay scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Federal scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Fern Bay scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Federal or Fern Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Federal has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
